summary: This is a brief post linking to O'Neil's Bloomberg View column where she critiques Yuval Harari's ideas about human-robot merger. The post signals skepticism toward transhumanist narratives, suggesting Harari's predictions are not to be taken seriously. O'Neil frames herself as unconvinced by the techno-utopian vision of humans merging with machines.
key_points: O'Neil is skeptical of Yuval Harari's predictions about human-technology merger
She characterizes Harari's ideas as potentially performative rather than sincere ('putting us all on')
The title signals personal resistance to transhumanist ideology
money_quotes: I'd Rather Not Merge With Robots, Thank You
Yuval Harari is putting us all on
key_insight: O'Neil dismisses transhumanist predictions about human-robot merger as hype that shouldn't be taken at face value.
